source.journal_name: Romanian Journal of Information Science and Technology

paper.title: Dimension-Reduced Modeling for Local Volatility Surface via Unsupervised Learning
paper.abstract: LVS dimension reduction via PCA and MPCA. Black-Scholes volatility smile. Tensor structure preservation. MARD validation shows 80% within 10%.

verdict.summary: Quantitative finance with established mathematical foundations. Black-Scholes equations (Eq. 1-6), Dupire's formula, PCA/MPCA tensor decomposition are all NECESSARY for volatility surface modeling. Level 4 mathematical finance - formalism is inherent to the domain.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 4 (Mathematical finance, stochastic calculus)

### Necessity Test
- Black-Scholes SDE (Eq. 1): NECESSARY - foundational model
- Call/Put pricing formulas (Eq. 2-3): NECESSARY - derived from SDE
- Gaussian kernel smoothing (Eq. 4): NECESSARY - standard technique for surface construction
- Dupire's equation (Eq. 6): NECESSARY - canonical formula for local volatility extraction
- PCA eigenvalue decomposition: NECESSARY - core methodology

**Verdict:** All formalism is intrinsic to mathematical finance. No theater detected.

paper.title: An Approach for Implementing Electronic Image Stabilization Using an FPGA System
paper.abstract: FPGA-based EIS implementation on Zynq UltraScale+ ZU7EV. Harris Corner Detector, Lucas-Kanade optical flow. 4K-UHD stabilization at 2.5x state of art.

verdict.summary: Hardware implementation paper with standard image processing formalism. Affine transformation matrix (Eq. 1-3) is NECESSARY for describing geometric transformations in image stabilization.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (FPGA design, image processing)

### Necessity Test
- Affine transformation matrix M (Eq. 1-2): NECESSARY - fundamental to image warping
- Coordinate transformation (Eq. 3): NECESSARY - describes pixel mapping
- Harris Corner detection, Lucas-Kanade: Standard computer vision algorithms (described procedurally)

**Verdict:** Minimal formalism, all appropriate for hardware implementation description.

paper.title: A Queuing Model to Evaluate the Network Performance Considering the Heterogeneity and Mobility of Relay Nodes in Disaster Area
paper.abstract: CTMC queuing model for H2H/M2M integrated traffic. Relay node mobility effects. Single buffer policy analysis. DES validation with <5% discrepancy.

verdict.summary: Queuing theory application with appropriate formalism. Continuous Time Markov Chains, state transition probabilities, and QoS metrics are NECESSARY for performance modeling. Table 1 abbreviations support mathematical notation used throughout.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3-4 (Queuing theory, stochastic processes)

### Necessity Test
- Arrival/departure rates (lambda, mu): NECESSARY - standard queuing notation
- Traffic load parameters: NECESSARY - performance analysis
- CTMC state transitions: NECESSARY - analytical modeling methodology
- Performance metrics (MQL, RT, BP): NECESSARY - standard QoS measures

**Verdict:** Queuing theory formalism is intrinsic to the methodology. Validated by DES.

paper.title: Deep Learning-Based Prediction Models for the Detection of Vitamin D Deficiency and 25-Hydroxyvitamin D Levels Using Complete Blood Count Tests
paper.abstract: DL models for VDD prediction from CBC tests. CNN, RNN, LSTM, GRU, Autoencoder comparison. ETC and Multi-task LASSO feature selection. 907 patients from Turkish lab.

flag_details.FORMALISM_THEATER: Level 2 applied ML classification problem. Table 1 (basic statistics) is appropriate, but paper lacks algorithmic necessity - standard DL architectures applied to tabular data. No novel mathematical contribution. Equations describing CNN/RNN layers are reproduced from textbooks without modification. 'Three separate models' framing inflates contribution for what is standard hyperparameter tuning.

verdict.summary: DISQUALIFIED: Applied ML classification with standard architectures. No novel formalism. DL equations are textbook reproductions applied to tabular medical data. Level 2 problem dressed with Level 3 terminology.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 2 (Applied ML classification - standard DL on tabular data)

### Necessity Test
- CNN, RNN, LSTM, GRU architectures: UNNECESSARY to restate - standard textbook algorithms
- Feature selection (ETC, LASSO): Standard preprocessing, not novel
- Three 'separate models': Actually same methodology with different labels

### Formalism Theater Patterns Detected
1. **Decorative Definitions**: DL layer equations reproduced without modification
2. **Inflated Contribution**: 'Three models' are trivial label variations
3. **Complexity Mismatch**: Level 2 problem (classification) with Level 3 presentation

**Verdict:** FORMALISM_THEATER - Disqualified.

paper.title: Statistical Computational Model of Fission Products Composition of Irradiated Nuclear Fuel and Their Contribution to Gas-aerosol Emissions of Nuclear Power Plants
paper.abstract: Radionuclide composition statistical analysis. Zipf-Mandelbrot distribution for rank-size modeling. VVER-440 reactor fuel analysis. 20 orders of magnitude variability.

verdict.summary: Nuclear physics statistical modeling. Radioactive decay equations (N(t), A(t)) are NECESSARY for activity calculations. Zipf-Mandelbrot distribution application is appropriate for rank-size analysis of radionuclides.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (Nuclear physics, statistical modeling)

### Necessity Test
- Radionuclide emission model (Eq. 1): NECESSARY - conservation of matter
- Decay equation N(t) = N0 * e^(-kt): NECESSARY - fundamental physics
- Zipf-Mandelbrot distribution: APPROPRIATE for rank-size analysis
- Fig. 1 demonstration model: Illustrative, not decorative

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism for nuclear/radiological modeling.

paper.title: Near-optimal March Tests for Three-Cell and Four-Cell Coupling Fault Models in Random-Access Memories
paper.abstract: Multirun march tests for RAM coupling faults. Eulerian graph coverage for near-optimality. 37n for 3-cell, 73n for 4-cell models. Extends unlinked static CF model.

verdict.summary: Memory testing theory with rigorous formalism. Fault primitives (Table 1), state graphs, Eulerian coverage are NECESSARY for proving test optimality. Level 4 hardware testing theory.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 4 (Memory testing theory, graph theory)

### Necessity Test
- Fault primitive notation (Table 1): NECESSARY - standard in memory test literature
- State/transition graphs: NECESSARY - optimality proofs require graph coverage
- Eulerian graph property: NECESSARY - defines near-optimality criterion
- Data background patterns (Fig. 3): NECESSARY - multirun testing methodology
- Observability conditions OC1, OC2: NECESSARY - formal test requirements

**Verdict:** All formalism is essential for proving test properties. Strong theoretical contribution.

paper.title: Network Traffic Prediction Performance Using LSTM
paper.abstract: LSTM for network traffic prediction. Activation function comparison (Sigmoid, Tanh, ReLU). Optimizer comparison (Adam, etc.). MAE, MAPE, MSE, RMSE, R-squared evaluation.

flag_details.FORMALISM_THEATER: Level 1-2 applied ML paper reproduces textbook LSTM equations (Eq. 1), activation functions (Eq. 2-4), and optimizer update rules without modification. No novel methodology - standard hyperparameter comparison. Equations are decorative definitions adding no scientific value. Sigmoid, Tanh, ReLU definitions are undergraduate-level material reproduced verbatim.

verdict.summary: DISQUALIFIED: Textbook equation reproduction as research contribution. LSTM gate equations (Eq. 1), activation function definitions (Eq. 2-4), Adam optimizer formula are standard material with no modification. Level 1-2 hyperparameter comparison dressed as research.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 1-2 (Applied ML - hyperparameter tuning exercise)

### Necessity Test
- LSTM equations (Eq. 1): UNNECESSARY - textbook reproduction, no modification
- Sigmoid function (Eq. 2): UNNECESSARY - undergraduate material
- Tanh function (Eq. 3): UNNECESSARY - undergraduate material  
- ReLU function (Eq. 4): UNNECESSARY - undergraduate material
- Adam optimizer (Eq. 5): UNNECESSARY - textbook reproduction

### Formalism Theater Patterns Detected
1. **Decorative Definitions**: All equations are textbook reproductions
2. **Set Notation for Lists**: Activation functions could be described in one sentence
3. **No Novel Contribution**: Comparing standard hyperparameters on standard data
4. **Complexity Mismatch**: Level 1-2 task with Level 3 formalism presentation

**Verdict:** FORMALISM_THEATER - Disqualified. Severe case.

paper.title: Stable Levitation of Single-point Levitation Systems for Maglev Trains by Improved Cascade Control
paper.abstract: Cascade control for EMS maglev. Lyapunov and sliding mode controllers for electromagnet system. PID with switching for motion system. Nonlinear system without linearization.

verdict.summary: Nonlinear control theory with appropriate formalism. Electromagnetic force equations, Lyapunov stability analysis, sliding mode controller design are NECESSARY for control system development. No linearization - handles nonlinear dynamics directly.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 4 (Nonlinear control theory)

### Necessity Test
- Electromagnetic system dynamics: NECESSARY - system model
- Lyapunov controller design: NECESSARY - stability proof methodology
- Sliding mode control: NECESSARY - robustness to disturbances
- Cascade control structure: NECESSARY - hierarchical control design
- Magnetic flux/force equations: NECESSARY - physics of levitation

**Verdict:** Control theory formalism is essential and well-applied.

paper.title: Design and Implementation of an Automated Instrument for Battery Charging Experiments
paper.abstract: Automated battery charge-discharge test instrument. Exponential current pulses during CC phase. Boost converter without output filter. Microcontroller-based monitoring.

verdict.summary: Instrumentation design with appropriate circuit analysis. RL circuit equations (Eq. 1-3) are NECESSARY for describing exponential pulse generation in boost converter topology. Time constant calculations required for design.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (Power electronics, instrumentation)

### Necessity Test
- Exponential current rise (Eq. 1): NECESSARY - describes coil charging dynamics
- Circuit resistance calculation (Eq. 2): NECESSARY - determines time constant
- Current decay through battery (Eq. 3): NECESSARY - describes discharge path
- Time constant tau_on, tau_off: NECESSARY - design parameters

**Verdict:** Standard circuit analysis formalism, appropriate for instrumentation design.

batch_notes: Volume 27 Issue 3-4 (2024) analyzed with RUIN Framework v2.0 and Necessity Test. **2 papers flagged FORMALISM_THEATER and DISQUALIFIED**: (1) paper783/yalda2024 - severe case of textbook equation reproduction as research (LSTM, activation functions); (2) paper780/essiz2024 - standard DL classification inflated as three 'separate models'. Remaining 7 papers show domain-appropriate formalism: mathematical finance (Level 4), hardware implementation, queuing theory, nuclear physics, memory testing theory (Level 4), nonlinear control (Level 4), instrumentation. Final: 1 CRITICAL, 1 LIMITED, 3 ADEQUATE, 4 STRONG. Disqualification rate: 22% (2/9).
